_comment: Task 99 recall-benchmark corpus. Memory-shaped facts for a fictional product team (de-biased from the kit's own domain, per the live-verify precedent). Each entry: key (stable handle queries reference), kind (fact|bullet), tier, type, title, body, trust, createdAt. Facts seed via the REAL writeFact path; bullets via memoryWrite-style scratchpad append. ~40 entries: ~16 query targets + ~24 plausible distractors so retrieval is non-trivial.

entries.title: pnpm standardization
entries.body: Standardized on pnpm for all workspace installs. npm and yarn are not used anywhere in the monorepo; the lockfile is pnpm-lock.yaml.

entries.title: Valkey caching layer
entries.body: Valkey is the caching layer for hot reads. Target latency SLA is 5ms p99; eviction policy is allkeys-lru.

entries.title: Primary database moved to CockroachDB
entries.body: Migrated the primary database from PostgreSQL to CockroachDB for geo-distribution. The platform team required multi-region writes; migration completed in late May.

entries.title: JWT bearer auth
entries.body: Authentication uses JWT bearer tokens with 15-minute expiry. Refresh tokens rotate on every use and are stored httpOnly.

entries.title: ruff before every commit
entries.body: Run ruff before every commit. CI fails the build on lint errors; no exceptions for WIP branches.

entries.title: project-local .venv via uv
entries.body: Always create a project-local .venv with uv. Never install packages globally; uv pip sync from the lockfile.

entries.title: Vitest test runner
entries.body: Vitest is the test runner. Every module gets boundary tests at its public interface; suites run via npm scripts only.

entries.title: Deploys via GitHub Actions to Fly.io
entries.body: Deploys go through GitHub Actions to Fly.io on every merge to main. Manual SSH deploys are forbidden; rollback is a redeploy of the previous image.

entries.title: Sentry for unhandled exceptions
entries.body: Sentry captures unhandled exceptions in production. Alerts route to the #ops channel; source maps upload on release.

entries.title: REST with JSON:API conventions
entries.body: The HTTP interface is REST following JSON:API conventions. GraphQL was evaluated and rejected; no schema stitching.

entries.title: squash-merge only
entries.body: Feature work branches off main. Squash-merge only; rebase-merges and merge commits are disabled in the repository settings.

entries.title: secrets in 1Password
entries.body: Secrets live in 1Password vaults and inject at deploy time. Never commit .env values; the repo ships .env.example only.

entries.title: UTC ISO-8601 timestamps
entries.body: All timestamps are stored as UTC ISO-8601. Conversion to the viewer's locale happens at the UI edge only; the backend never localizes.

entries.title: React 19 + Vite web client
entries.body: The web client is React 19 built with Vite. Styling is Tailwind utility classes; no CSS-in-JS.

entries.title: exponential backoff for external calls
entries.body: External HTTP calls use exponential backoff with jitter, maximum 5 attempts. The circuit breaker opens after 3 consecutive failures and half-opens after 30 seconds.

entries.text: moving CI onto the GitHub merge queue so main stays green under parallel landings
prefers small PRs under 400 lines; large diffs get split before review

entries.title: Playwright e2e
entries.body: End-to-end tests run on Playwright against the staging environment, nightly.

entries.title: Docker Compose local dev
entries.body: Local development runs through Docker Compose; one command brings up the API, worker, and database.

entries.title: API rate limit
entries.body: Public API rate limit is 100 requests per minute per token, enforced at the gateway.

entries.title: avatars in S3
entries.body: User avatars are stored in S3 with a CloudFront CDN in front; uploads are pre-signed.

entries.title: i18next localization
entries.body: Localization uses i18next with JSON message catalogs per locale; English is the source language.

entries.title: LaunchDarkly feature flags
entries.body: Feature flags are managed in LaunchDarkly; kill switches exist for every new surface.

entries.title: analytics warehouse
entries.body: The analytics warehouse is a separate PostgreSQL instance fed by nightly ETL; product data never queries it synchronously.

entries.title: systemd timers
entries.body: Scheduled jobs run via systemd timers on the worker host, not in-process schedulers.

entries.title: Postmark transactional email
entries.body: Transactional email goes through Postmark with templated streams; marketing email is a separate system.

entries.title: legacy Elasticsearch
entries.body: The legacy application used Elasticsearch for product search; it is read-only and scheduled for decommission.

entries.title: React Native mobile app
entries.body: The mobile app is React Native sharing the design system tokens, released through the app stores monthly.

entries.title: retired chat prototype
entries.body: The old chat prototype used Redis pub/sub for fanout; it was retired and the pattern is not used anywhere current.

entries.title: Turborepo task graph
entries.body: The monorepo task graph is orchestrated by Turborepo; remote caching is enabled in CI.

entries.title: OpenAPI spec generation
entries.body: The OpenAPI document is generated from route annotations on every build and published to the developer portal.

entries.title: database backups
entries.body: Database backups run hourly with 30-day retention; restore drills happen quarterly.

entries.title: on-call rotation
entries.body: On-call rotates weekly through the platform team; handoff notes are mandatory at rotation boundaries.

entries.title: design tokens
entries.body: Design tokens are the single source for color and spacing; raw hex values fail the style linter.

entries.title: Storybook component catalog
entries.body: Every shared component ships a Storybook story; visual regression runs on Chromatic.

entries.title: gRPC evaluation
entries.body: gRPC was evaluated for service-to-service calls and parked; the team stayed on plain HTTP until there are more internal services.

entries.title: background jobs on SQS
entries.body: Background jobs ride SQS with a dead-letter queue; consumers are idempotent by contract.

entries.title: pairing cadence
entries.body: Pairs on gnarly debugging sessions but prefers solo deep work for feature build-out.

entries.title: editor setup
entries.body: Works in VS Code with vim keybindings; terminal is Windows Terminal with git-bash.

entries.text: whether to move standup to async written updates twice a week
